claim: Enterprises are shifting from renting generic AI (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini APIs) to building owned, specialized models—quality 2-10x higher, cost 10-100x lower
actionable: For high-volume use cases, measure ROI of fine-tuning vs. API calls; target 1M+ calls/month for fine-tuning to break even
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Manos Koukoumidis & Stefan Webb: VibeML: Build your AI model in hours, not months

claim: VibeML agent automates full model-building cycle: define task → generate evaluators → synthesize data → train → evaluate → iterate; takes hours, not months
actionable: Use VibeML or similar agentic fine-tuning tools; start with natural language task description; let system generate metrics and training data
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Manos Koukoumidis & Stefan Webb: VibeML: Build your AI model in hours, not months

claim: Review failure modes feature: analyze where baseline fails, synthesize targeted training data for those cases, iterate—compound improvement loop
actionable: After baseline evaluation, inspect failure mode categories; generate synthetic data for edge cases; retrain with new data
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Manos Koukoumidis & Stefan Webb: VibeML: Build your AI model in hours, not months

claim: New York Times study: custom faithfulness model built with VibeML outperforms GPT-4 on hallucination detection; only 39% of Gemini 3 overviews fully supported by sources
actionable: Build task-specific evaluators rather than relying on generic models; fine-tuned judges outperform larger generic models on domain-specific metrics
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Manos Koukoumidis & Stefan Webb: VibeML: Build your AI model in hours, not months

claim: LoRA fine-tuning is parameter-efficient; full weight fine-tuning gives more flexibility; trade off compute cost vs. model quality in VibeML UI
actionable: Start with LoRA for speed; use full fine-tuning if LoRA underperforms; measure validation metrics to choose
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Manos Koukoumidis & Stefan Webb: VibeML: Build your AI model in hours, not months
